name: Receipts
_concept: Brutalist editorial. Monospace as body co-equal. Pure achromatic neutrals — no warm cream, no hue. The only color anywhere is reserved for verdict semantics and severity tags. The verdict IS the color story.

bg-0.role: Page canvas, deepest. Cooler than Direction A — hue stripped to 0.

ink._note: Pure achromatic. No oklch hue. Reads as cooler / more clinical than Direction A's tinted neutrals.

accent._REMOVED: No brand accent. Warm cream is removed. Color is reserved for verdict semantics and severity only. This rule is the brand.

verdict._note: PRD-locked (§7.2 Step D). In Direction C, verdict colors carry MORE weight because they are the ONLY colors on the page.

fail.role: FAIL verdict — the dominant color event in the product

severity._note: Severity tags continue the verdict palette logic — these are the only non-neutral pixels in the brand outside verdict states.

ink-3_on_bg-0._use: Large text or non-text UI components only. Decoration.

typography._concept: Geist Mono is promoted from accent to body co-equal. ~50% of all type in the product is mono. Sans handles paragraphs and buttons. Instrument Serif retreats to italicized H1 phrases and verdict-screen headlines only.

serif.role: Italicized phrases in H1 only. Verdict screen verdict-line headline. Nowhere else.

sans.role: Paragraph body, button labels, long-form prose only. Less than half the type in the product.

mono.role: BODY CO-EQUAL. Used for: all navigation, all metadata, all numbers, all data labels, all status indicators, all severity tags, all eyebrows, all timestamps, all file paths, all command examples, all in-app row metadata, footer, captions, in-product side panels. Reads as terminal / changelog / data-table register.

display-xl._note: Tightened letter-spacing -0.04em vs Direction A's -0.035em. Brutalist register packs tighter.

h-card._note: Smaller than A's 38px — Direction C's dense register dials display down

h-step._note: Step titles are MONO in Direction C. Changelog register.

mono-body._use: Mono body for in-app rows, sidebar contents, metadata-heavy regions

mono-meta._note: Eyebrows; lower letter-spacing than A (0.06 vs 0.08) — less editorial-formal, more terminal

mono-large._use: Large numeric callouts — score numerator, stat blocks. Replaces Direction A's serif-italic stat.

spacing._note: Tighter scale than Direction A (drops 18, 28, 36, 100, 140). Brutalist register tightens vertical rhythm.
spacing._wrap_note: Slightly wider than A (1320 vs 1280) — brutalist register tolerates dense rows

radius._rule: 2px on surfaces (soft brutalism — not literal sharp corners, but very close). 0px on chips and tags. NO pill radius anywhere — pills read as 'SaaS' in this register. Buttons use 2px radius.

motion._concept: Aggressive motion reduction by default. Static-first. Brutalist register treats motion as a feature interaction, not as decoration.

rules.marquee: DISABLED — no auto-marquee in Direction C

prefers_reduced_motion.rule: All durations collapse to <=80ms. All transforms disabled.
prefers_reduced_motion.implementation: Same media query as Directions A/B.

elevation._note: Direction C is FLAT. Zero drop-shadow. Elevation lives in hairlines only.

grain._note: Reduced from A's 0.035 — Direction C is cleaner. Grain present as texture insurance against pure-flat panels reading as 'rendered low-quality.'

verdict_surface._amplified: In Direction C, the verdict screen is the SINGULAR color event in the brand experience. The visual contrast is intentional — the achromatic product surrounds and contextualizes the verdict.
verdict_surface.fail_frame: 1px solid #5a2828 on bg-0; chip = #C8421A fill, #fafafa text. The chip can be MUCH larger than in A/B — fills the upper hero of the verdict screen.
verdict_surface.pwf_frame: 1px solid #5a3f1e on bg-0; chip = #E4C875 fill, #050507 text
verdict_surface.pass_frame: 1px solid var(--line-3) on bg-0; chip = #14C8CC fill, #050507 text

_direction_c_invariants: There is no brand-warm accent. There is no brand-blue. There is no brand-gradient.
The only chromatic events on the page are verdict states and severity tags. Everything else is neutral.
Geist Mono is used for at least 40% of rendered type at any viewport. If a screen has <40% mono, it has drifted from the brand.
No pill radius anywhere. All chips and tags are 0px radius (true sharp). All cards / buttons / inputs are 2px radius.
No drop-shadows. No glows. No gradients on surfaces (verdict-chip-fill is the single exception).
No auto-playing motion. No marquee. No ring pulses. Hover transitions are <=180ms.
Cursor-glow is removed. Grain is reduced. The product is quiet.
